why_winner: Angle B opens with the sharpest, most provocative hook for a developer audience — directly challenging a tool they already know (GitHub Actions) is more arresting than a scene-setter or a how-to lead. The 'scripts that follow rules vs agents that reason' contrast is the post's core insight and B surfaces it earliest, then builds to the practical payoff. Performance context also favors this: bold claim-first on developer tool comparisons is the highest-engagement angle in the existing tracker data (5.09% on claude-vs-codex).

runner_up_notes.A: The scene-setting in Angle A ('Close your laptop. Your AI is still running.') is more evocative than B's equivalent. B already incorporates the 'Until now, if you closed your laptop, your AI automation died' line which carries the same weight. No additional grafting needed — the key asset is already present in B.
runner_up_notes.C: The prompt anatomy breakdown (Role, Task, Steps, Output, Constraints, Error Handling) is genuinely useful and could be a follow-up post on its own. Not incorporated into the winner — it would bloat B and dilute the core contrast argument.
